title: Implement TUI status, mesh, config, and diagnostics views
description: Why this exists: the TUI should be a useful field and development tool, not only a chat window. What needs to be done: implement node status, radio/link summary, BLE/IP connection info, mesh neighbor or route state, basic editable config fields, key/trust status display where safe, log subscription, raw diagnostics, and unsupported-feature messaging. Acceptance criteria: status/config/mesh/log screens use the shared model; sensitive key material is never printed accidentally; config writes require confirmation; unsupported firmware operations show actionable errors; tests cover render and command behavior.
notes:
Implementation evidence for TUI status/config/mesh/log/diagnostics views:
- Added data-backed Dashboard, Nodes, Mesh, Config, Logs, and Diagnostics view state using the shared Python LCI client model.
- Dashboard refresh calls get_status/get_config/get_identity/discover, updates device/battery status bar fields, and renders status/radio/DODAG/resource summaries.
- Mesh refresh calls list_neighbors/list_routes and renders neighbor RSSI/SNR/ETX/trust plus route prefix/via/metric/lifetime rows.
- Config refresh calls get_config/get_radio_config/get_identity, renders mutable/read-only rows, redacts key-like identity material, and adds a user-reachable edit flow: / focuses field/value inputs, Enter stages allowed fields, Enter opens confirmation, y or Enter writes. Writes are allowlisted to name/role and mutable radio fields; read-only/sensitive/unknown fields are rejected before transport calls.
- Config write errors clear pending state and remain visible until cancelled/edited; invalid numeric input is recoverable.
- Logs view starts a live /logs Observe task with normalized log rows and closes subscription on app unmount.
- Diagnostics refresh flattens decoded payloads deterministically and redacts key/raw/payload names plus bytes-like values.
- Shared text inputs are restored when leaving Config edit so config field/value text cannot leak into chat drafts.

Local validation:
- cd python && uv run ruff check src/lichen/tui tests/tui pyproject.toml -> passed.
- cd python && uv run mypy src/lichen/tui tests/tui -> passed, no issues in 4 source files.
- cd python && uv run pytest tests/tui tests/client -q -> 73 passed in 6.86s.
- git diff --check -> passed.

Code review:
- Fermat final re-review: no P0/P1/P2 findings after input restoration and config write allowlist.
- Bernoulli final re-review: no blocking P0/P1/P2 findings after stale pending config write fix.

No EC2 used for this Python-only task.
